skill.name: elevenlabs-music-generation
skill.description: Generate full songs and instrumental tracks with ElevenLabs Music on RunComfy via the runcomfy CLI. ElevenLabs Music turns a style description plus structured lyrics into studio-quality 44.1 kHz stereo audio from 5 seconds to 5 minutes with section-level control, multilingual vocals, and commercial-friendly output.
skill.summary: Generate full songs and instrumental tracks using ElevenLabs AI through the RunComfy CLI.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 93 potential issues, but all are false positives. SKILL.md is a markdown documentation file, not executable code. Backtick syntax in code blocks is documentation formatting. Hardcoded URLs are legitimate RunComfy API endpoints. No cryptographic algorithms or malicious patterns exist. The skill properly documents shell injection prevention via JSON input and uses standard token storage with mode 0600 permissions.

medium_findings.title: External Command Patterns in Documentation
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 69 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md. These are markdown code blocks containing example CLI commands. The backticks are documentation formatting, not executable code. SKILL.md is a documentation file that describes how to use the runcomfy CLI - it does not execute any commands itself. This is a false positive from the static analyzer misinterpreting markdown documentation as code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is SKILL.md - a markdown documentation file. All backticks appear inside fenced code blocks (```) which are documentation examples, not executable code. The skill invokes the CLI as a single command without shell expansion of user input.

medium_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs for External Service
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 12 instances of 'Hardcoded URL' at lines 19, 27, 51, 74, 166, 184-188. These are legitimate RunComfy API endpoints and documentation links. As a music generation skill that calls the RunComfy Model API, external URLs are expected and necessary for the skill to function. The skill explicitly documents its outbound endpoints as model-api.runcomfy.net and download whitelisted domains.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill is a music generation tool that requires calling the RunComfy API. URLs are documented service endpoints, not hidden exfiltration channels.

low_findings.title: API Token Storage Path
low_findings.description: Line 175 references ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json for token storage. This is standard practice for CLI tools and is properly documented with mode 0600 permissions and RUNCOMFY_TOKEN env var alternative for CI environments. This is not a security issue.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Token storage at ~/.config with mode 0600 is standard secure practice. The skill explicitly warns against echoing tokens or checking them in.

content.user_title: Generate Professional Music Tracks with AI
content.value_statement: Creating custom music for videos, podcasts, and games requires expensive studio time. ElevenLabs Music through RunComfy generates studio-quality songs and instrumentals from text descriptions in minutes.
actual_capabilities: Generate full vocal songs with verse, chorus, and bridge structure
Create instrumental tracks for podcasts, games, and videos
Produce multilingual music with lyrics in any language
Generate tracks from 5 seconds to 5 minutes
Create branded jingles and stinger sounds
limitations: Cannot generate sound effects or single audio cues
Cannot specify or clone specific vocal voices
Requires paid RunComfy API account for generation
Tracks over 5 minutes require external stitching

use_cases.title: Video Content Creator
use_cases.description: Quickly generate background music and theme songs for YouTube videos, social media clips, and presentations without hiring composers or licensing stock music.

use_cases.title: Podcast Producer
use_cases.description: Create custom intro and outro music, ambient beds, and transition sounds that match your podcast brand identity.

use_cases.title: Game Developer
use_cases.description: Generate looping background tracks and ambient sounds for indie games without music production expertise.

prompt_templates.title: Generate an instrumental track
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an instrumental track for [use case]. Style: [genre description]. Include: [instruments]. Tempo: [BPM]. Duration: [length].
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need background music without vocals

prompt_templates.title: Create a full song with lyrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a [genre] song titled [title]. Style: [mood and instruments]. [Intro bars] [Verse lyrics] [Chorus lyrics] [Bridge section] [Outro]. Duration: [length].
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a complete song with structure and vocals

prompt_templates.title: Make a short brand jingle
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a [duration] brand stinger. Style: [energetic/calm/upbeat]. Instruments: [specific sounds]. No vocals. End on a clear resolution.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a quick logo sound or transition

prompt_templates.title: Iterate on a music direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a draft track at [short duration] with [style description]. Then based on this draft, generate a full [length] version with [specific changes].
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to test an idea before committing to full production

output_examples.input: Generate a calming lo-fi instrumental for a study playlist. Warm piano, soft drums, 80 BPM, no vocals.
output_examples.output: A 90-second ambient lo-fi track with warm Rhodes piano, mellow drum beat, and clean ending suitable for background listening during focused work.

output_examples.input: Create an upbeat pop anthem with a female lead vocal. Include intro, verse, chorus, and bridge sections.
output_examples.output: A 3-minute pop song featuring a female vocalist with verse-chorus structure, driving drums, electric guitars, and a soft piano bridge section.

output_examples.input: Make a 10-second cheerful brand stinger with marimba.
output_examples.output: A quick 5-second audio clip ending on an uplifting chord resolve, featuring bright marimba tones and no vocals.

best_practices: Start with a short draft (30-45 seconds) to validate the music direction before generating a full-length track
Include specific genre, tempo, and instrument descriptions to guide the AI toward your desired sound
Use section markers like [Intro], [Verse], [Chorus], [Bridge] to structure vocal songs with clear structural intent
anti_patterns: Do not include contradictory style instructions like 'aggressive metal' and 'soft lullaby' in the same prompt
Do not use this skill for sound effects like thunder or footsteps; use a dedicated sound effects tool instead
Do not expect specific voice identities; force_instrumental is the only vocal control available

faq.question: What is the cost of generating music?
faq.answer: Approximately $0.0083 per second of generated audio. A 30-second track costs about $0.25 and a 5-minute track costs about $2.49.

faq.question: Can I generate music longer than 5 minutes?
faq.answer: The maximum duration per API call is 5 minutes. For longer pieces, generate separate sections and stitch them together using audio editing software.

faq.question: How do I create instrumental-only tracks?
faq.answer: Set force_instrumental: true in your request AND include 'no vocals' in your prompt text for double confirmation.

faq.question: Can I specify a particular singer's voice?
faq.answer: No, the force_instrumental flag is the only vocal control. Specific voice identities or voice cloning are not available through this endpoint.

faq.question: Is generated music royalty-free for commercial use?
faq.answer: Yes, ElevenLabs Music output is commercial-friendly. However, you are responsible for ensuring any lyrics you provide do not infringe on third-party copyrights.

faq.question: What audio format does the skill output?
faq.answer: By default, output is MP3 standard format at 44.1 kHz stereo quality. WAV format is also available as an option.
